chunk_summary: This section explores adolescent-specific barriers to nutritional adherence in chronic cardiorespiratory diseases, focusing on body image concerns and the development of transition skills for independent care management.
learning_objectives: Understand the impact of body image on nutritional adherence in adolescents with chronic cardiorespiratory diseases.
Identify strategies to improve transition skills for self-management of nutrition in adolescents.
Review evidence-based guidelines for adolescent nutrition in chronic conditions like cystic fibrosis.
Analyze the role of pancreatic enzyme supplementation in adolescent nutrition care.
key_facts: Adolescents aged 10–19 years require tailored nutritional strategies to address growth, body image, and disease-specific needs (Ref. 1).
ESPEN-ESPGHAN-ECFS guidelines emphasize the importance of high-calorie diets and pancreatic enzyme supplementation for cystic fibrosis patients (Ref. 2).
Transition skills are critical for adolescents to manage their own nutritional care as they approach adulthood (Ref. 3).
Body image concerns can significantly impact adherence to prescribed nutritional regimens in chronic diseases (Ref. 1).
Pancreatic enzyme supplementation is essential for nutrient absorption in cystic fibrosis patients, as per Cystic Fibrosis Foundation guidelines (Ref. 4).
reference_quote: Adolescents aged 10–19 years require tailored nutritional strategies to address growth, body image, and disease-specific needs. (Ref. 1)
document_key_topics: Adolescent nutrition in chronic cardiorespiratory diseases
Pancreatic enzyme supplementation in cystic fibrosis
